title: Iridescent City Forge — Post-Process Alchemy
description: A miniature VirtualCity sits on a table beside a glowing PotOfCoals. The user forges iridescent shield domes (derived from the IridescenceAbalone shell) over city districts by scooping energy from the coals and painting post-processing effects onto each district. Effects are governed by a conservation law: a fixed global 'energy budget' is split among bloom, chromatic aberration, vignette, tone-mapping exposure, and film grain across all districts, so boosting one district's effect reduces what remains for others. Full undo/redo history is required.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with the following setup. Load VirtualCity.glb centered at origin scaled to fit a 6-unit bounding box; it represents the main scene. Place PotOfCoals.glb to the right of the city (x+4) scaled 0.6×, with a pulsing PointLight (orange, intensity oscillates via sin) inside to simulate glowing embers. Load IridescenceAbalone.glb but clone its geometry 6 times as translucent iridescent dome meshes (MeshPhysicalMaterial, iridescence=1, transparent, opacity 0.35) that will hover above 6 city districts (subdivide city bounding box into a 2×3 grid). Use EffectComposer with passes: UnrealBloomPass, ShaderPass for chromatic aberration, ShaderPass for vignette, ShaderPass for film grain, and adaptive tone-mapping exposure uniform. Implement a global energy budget of 100 units shared across all 6 districts and 5 effect channels. Clicking a dome selects that district (highlight ring). A right-side HTML panel shows 5 sliders (bloom, chromaticAberration, vignette, exposure, grain) for the selected district. Adjusting any slider consumes budget; if budget is exhausted the slider clamps. Each district stores its own 5 values; the composer blends them weighted by screen-space mask (use stencil or depth layers per district). Interaction steps: (1) click dome to select district, (2-6) adjust up to 5 sliders distributing energy, (7) click PotOfCoals to 'recharge' budget by 10 (max once per district, coals flare animation), (8) click abalone shell reference object in corner to toggle iridescence visibility on all domes, (9) Ctrl+Z for undo restoring previous slider state and refunding budget, (10) Ctrl+Y for redo. Camera: OrbitControls, initial position (0,8,12) looking at origin. Add ambient light 0.3 and directional light from top-left. Display remaining budget as a top-center HTML overlay bar that changes color from green to red as budget depletes. Each undo/redo step must correctly conserve total energy. All post-process uniforms update in the render loop based on combined district values.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  energyBudget: number,         // remaining global energy units (0-100)
  selectedDistrictIndex: number,         // currently selected district (0-5, -1 if none)
  totalEffectIntensity: number,         // sum of all effect values across all districts
  coalLightIntensity: number,         // current pulsing intensity of coal pot light (0-2)
  domeOpacityAverage: number,         // average opacity of all 6 iridescent domes (0-1)
  bloomContribution: number,         // weighted bloom contribution from all districts (0-10)
  chromaticAberrationContribution: number,         // weighted chromatic aberration (0-0.1)
  vignetteContribution: number,         // weighted vignette darkness (0-2)
  exposureContribution: number,         // weighted tone-mapping exposure (0-3)
  grainContribution: number,         // weighted film grain intensity (0-1)
  districtEffectCount: number,         // count of districts with non-zero effects (0-6)
  isStable: boolean,         // true if no slider changes for 3+ seconds
}
